course_description.overview: Challenging road course around Bergamo featuring significant climbs in the Lombardy hills, honoring Felice Gimondi with routes through iconic cycling territory.

key_segments.name: Selvino climb
key_segments.description: Steep ascent testing endurance early in the route.

key_segments.name: Orobie foothills
key_segments.description: Rolling hills with panoramic views of Bergamo plains.

key_segments.name: Final circuits
key_segments.description: Technical descents and punchy rises near finish.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Demands strong climbing ability with sustained efforts and variable gradients; suitable for experienced gran fondo riders.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into BGY Milan Bergamo Airport (5 km from start), trains to Bergamo station, or drive via A4 highway.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels in Bergamo centro storico or near Orio al Serio; book early for May event.
logistics.registration_details: Online via official site granfondobgy.it, early bird fees around 50-70 EUR, multiple distances available.
logistics.bike_logistics: Bike assembly at event expo, mechanics on-site, transport via team cars if needed.
logistics.local_considerations: Expect spring weather 15-22C, possible rain; roads closed for event, Italian traffic rules apply.

final_verdict.one_liner: Prestigious Bergamo classic with legacy climbs and passionate community.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Ambitious road cyclists seeking Italian gran fondo heritage and challenging hills.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for its history, organization, and value in cycling's heartland.
pro_tips: Stay in Bergamo for culture and pasta carbo-load.
Pack rain gear for Lombardy May weather.
